pattern: A cell lies on an X exactly when its row equals its column or they sum to n-1, so the diagonal cells must be non-zero and every other cell must be zero.
hints: Each cell is either on one of the two diagonals or it is not; the rule differs for the two groups.
A cell (r, c) is on a diagonal when r == c or r + c == n - 1.
Scan every cell: if it is on a diagonal it must be non-zero, otherwise it must be zero; any violation makes the answer false.

edge_inputs.description: center counted once must be non-zero in odd size
even size where the two diagonals never meet

stress.description: build a genuine X-matrix then randomly corrupt one cell half the time
